docs.title: RideSheet
docs.text:
Welcome to RideSheet! RideSheet is a simple and affordable tool for operating and reporting on small demand-response transportation services. It supports rural, small urban, and urban specialized services that are ready to move away from paper, whiteboards, and ad-hoc spreadsheets and toward systematic and accessible tracking tools.

Getting started User Guide

docs.title: Features
docs.text:
• Built on Google Cloud Infrastructure Because RideSheet is in the cloud, you benefit from features like automatic backups and version tracking, easy collaboration across your organization, and data security that meets HIPAA regulations.
  • Spreadsheet-Based Anyone with experience in Excel, Google Sheets, or other spreadsheet applications will find it easy to learn RideSheet. Plus, it’s flexible and easy to customize.
  • Manages Tracking, Manifests, and Reports RideSheet handles tracking customers, trips, runs and more, and makes it easy to generate manifests and reports. It is customizable to handle thorny problems such as splitting trips between multiple funding sources.

Learn more about RideSheet and if it is right for your organization.

For developers and technical users, see our Technical Guide or check out RideSheet on GitHub.

RideSheet © 2026 Full Path LLC is Open Source software available under the MIT License.

docs.title: Getting Started
docs.text:
This documentation will guide you through the process of getting started with RideSheet. Existing users should refer to the User Guide.

RideSheet User Group

The RideSheet User Group meets monthly over Zoom and can provide peer support for new and existing RideSheet users. Email ridesheet-info@fullpath.io to join.

docs.title: What You Need to Use RideSheet
docs.text:
Before diving into RideSheet, make sure you have the following:

  • A Google Workspace account: RideSheet is based on Google Sheets and requires a Google Workspace account for hosting.
  • A reliable internet connection: Since RideSheet operates online, a stable internet connection is necessary for accessing and updating your data.
  • Basic spreadsheet skills: While RideSheet simplifies many tasks, familiarity with spreadsheet software, particularly Google Sheets, will be helpful.

Google for Nonprofits

For many nonprofit organizations, Google provides free or discounted access to its services through the Google for Nonprofits program. Learn more about eligibility requirements.

docs.title: What You Need to Set Up RideSheet
Technical Skills
docs.text:
Setting up RideSheet requires basic technical skills, especially if you choose to do it yourself. You should be comfortable with:

  • Creating and managing Google Sheets
  • Understanding basic spreadsheet functions
  • Configuring Google Workspace settings

Ideally, you will also have some experience using:

  • The command line
  • GitHub
  • Basic JavaScript

If you feel comfortable with these skills and would like to learn more or set up a demo sheet to explore RideSheet's capabilities, see the guide to installing RideSheet.

docs.title: Getting Help Installing/Setting Up RideSheet
docs.text:
If you're not confident in setting up RideSheet on your own, don't worry. Most small agencies will not have the resources or technical expertise on hand to set up RideSheet independently. You can:

  • Hire a technical consultant: Consider hiring a consultant with experience in setting up Google Workspace and spreadsheet-based solutions.
  • Seek help from Full Path: Contact us for guidance on installation and setup. In some cases, there may be grant-based assistance available to help you get started.

docs.title: Creating a Plan for Migrating to RideSheet
docs.text: Migrating to RideSheet requires careful planning to ensure a smooth transition. Consider the following:

docs.title: Moving Data
docs.text:
Transfer existing data from paper records or other digital systems to RideSheet. This may involve:

  • Importing data into Google Sheets
  • Formatting data to fit RideSheet's structure

docs.title: Training Staff
docs.text: Prepare your team for using RideSheet effectively, and plan for a temporary loss of productivity as staff acclimate to a new tool. If you are working with FullPath to get RideSheet set up for your organization, we will help you conduct training sessions and get started.

docs.title: Security Best Practices
docs.text:
Protect your organization's data by implementing security best practices, such as:

  • Enabling two-factor authentication
  • Restricting access to sensitive information
  • Using a password manager

docs.title: Determining Customizations
docs.text:
Assess if any customizations are necessary to align RideSheet with your organization's specific needs. This could involve:

  • Adding custom fields to track unique data
  • Modifying report templates

docs.title: Maintenance and Ongoing Costs
docs.text:
While RideSheet offers a cost-effective solution, consider ongoing maintenance and costs, including:

  • Regular backups of data to prevent loss
  • Software updates to access new features and security patches
  • Budgeting for technical support and assistance as needed

With careful planning and attention to detail, RideSheet can streamline your DRT service management, making operations more efficient and organized.

docs.title: RideSheet Privacy Policy
docs.text:
Service Provider: Full Path LLC

Last Updated: December 31, 2025

docs.title: Overview
docs.text: RideSheet is an open-source transportation management tool developed and maintained by Full Path LLC. This tool operates entirely within your Google account. This privacy policy explains what data RideSheet accesses, how it's used, and where it's stored.

docs.title: Who Provides RideSheet
docs.text:
In this privacy policy, "we," "us," and "our" refer to Full Path LLC, the developer and maintainer of RideSheet.

Key Points:

  • All your data stays in your Google account
  • RideSheet does not upload your data to external servers
  • RideSheet does not share your data with third parties
  • You control all permissions and can revoke them at any time
  • All code is open-source and publicly reviewable

docs.title: Information RideSheet Accesses
docs.text: When you grant permissions, RideSheet can access:

docs.title: 1. Your RideSheet Spreadsheet
docs.text:
• Customer information (names, addresses, phone numbers)
  • Trip details (pickup/dropoff locations, times, dates)
  • Driver and vehicle information
  • Run schedules and assignments
  • Post-service data for reporting and analysis of operations
  • Any other data you enter into the spreadsheet

docs.title: 2. Google Docs You Specify
docs.text:
• Your manifest template document (the specific document you configure in settings)
  • Driver manifest documents that RideSheet creates

docs.title: 3. Files RideSheet Creates
docs.text: • Driver manifest documents (Google Docs and PDFs)

docs.title: 4. Google Maps Data
docs.text:
• Address validation when you enter addresses
  • Driving directions and distance calculations
  • Plus Code validation

docs.title: How RideSheet Uses Your Information
docs.text: RideSheet uses your information solely to provide transportation management functionality:

docs.title: Core Functions
docs.text:
• Data management: Store and organize customer, trip, driver, and vehicle information
  • Calculations: Calculate trip distances, travel times, and estimates
  • Geocoding: Convert addresses to coordinates for mapping
  • Manifest generation: Create formatted driver manifests from your trip data
  • Automation: Auto-fill fields, validate data, and maintain data integrity

docs.title: What RideSheet Does NOT Do
docs.text:
• Store your data on external servers
  • Share your data with third parties
  • Sell your data
  • Use your data for analytics or marketing
  • Access your data when you're not actively using RideSheet
  • Track your usage patterns

docs.title: Where Your Data Is Stored
docs.text:
All your data stays in your Google account:

  • Trip data is stored in your RideSheet spreadsheet
  • Generated manifests are stored in your Google Drive folders
  • Nothing is stored outside your Google account

Exception: When you use address validation or distance calculation features, addresses are sent to Google Maps API. This is Google's own service, and the interaction is the same as if you searched for those addresses and trips in Google Maps directly.

docs.title: Services RideSheet Connects To
docs.text:
1. Google Maps API

    • Purpose: Geocode addresses, calculate distances
    • Data sent: Addresses you enter in the spreadsheet. Only address strings are sent to the Google Maps API for geocoding and distance calculation. No personally identifiable information (PII) such as passenger names or phone numbers is shared with the Maps service.
    • Privacy: Covered by Google's privacy policy

docs.title: No Other Third Parties
docs.text:
RideSheet does not:

  • Share data with advertising networks
  • Integrate with social media platforms
  • Send data to analytics services
  • Use third-party tracking tools
  • Share data for research purposes

docs.title: Permission Control
docs.text:
• You choose which permissions to grant individually
  • You can revoke permissions at any time on per-user basis at https://myaccount.google.com/connections
  • Revoking permissions immediately removes RideSheet's access for your individual user account

docs.title: Data Control
docs.text:
• You own all your data
  • You can delete data anytime by deleting spreadsheet rows or files
  • You can export your data using Google's standard export tools
  • You can uninstall RideSheet by deleting the spreadsheet

docs.title: No Account Required
docs.text: RideSheet doesn't require creating a separate account. It uses your existing Google account authentication.

docs.title: Code Visibility
docs.text:
• All RideSheet code is publicly available on GitHub at https://github.com/full-path/ridesheet
  • You can review exactly what the code does
  • You can verify that it follows this privacy policy
  • Community members can audit the code for security and privacy

docs.title: No Hidden Functions
docs.text: Because the code is open-source, there are no hidden data collection mechanisms or undisclosed features.

docs.title: Your Responsibility
docs.text:
• RideSheet operates under your Google account's security
  • Follow Google's security best practices (strong passwords, 2FA)
  • Limit spreadsheet sharing to authorized personnel only
  • Review who has access to your Drive folders

For maximum data control and security, we strongly recommend you only store sensitive data in organizational Google Workspace accounts.

docs.title: RideSheet's Security Practices
docs.text:
• Minimal permission requests (most restrictive scopes available)
  • No data storage on external servers (no server to breach)
  • No password storage (uses Google authentication)
  • Open-source code (community security review)

docs.title: Children's Privacy
docs.text: RideSheet is not directed at children under 13. We do not knowingly collect information from children. The transportation data you manage may include information about minor passengers, which you are responsible for handling in compliance with applicable privacy laws.

docs.title: Changes to This Privacy Policy
docs.text:
We may update this privacy policy to reflect:

  • Changes in privacy laws or regulations
  • Changes to RideSheet's functionality
  • Improvements to privacy practices

When we update this policy:

  • The "Last Updated" date will change
  • Material changes will be noted in the RideSheet release notes
  • Continued use of RideSheet constitutes acceptance of the updated policy

docs.title: GDPR (European Users)
docs.text:
If you're in the EU/EEA, you have additional rights:

  • Right to access your data (it's in your Google account)
  • Right to rectification (edit your spreadsheet)
  • Right to erasure (delete rows or revoke permissions)
  • Right to data portability (export from Google Sheets)
  • Right to object (revoke permissions)

docs.title: CCPA (California Users)
docs.text:
If you're in California, you have rights to:

  • Know what data is collected (listed above)
  • Delete your data (delete spreadsheet/revoke permissions)
  • Opt-out of data sale (RideSheet doesn't sell data)

docs.title: HIPAA
docs.text: Important: RideSheet is not HIPAA-compliant out of the box. Do not use RideSheet to store Protected Health Information (PHI) unless you have adequate measures in place for HIPAA compliance.

docs.title: Contact Information
docs.text:
For privacy questions or concerns, contact Full Path LLC:

  • Email: info@ridesheet.org
  • GitHub Issues: https://github.com/full-path/ridesheet/issues
  • Response time: We aim to respond within 5 business days

For data removal requests:

Revoke RideSheet's permissions for each user with access at https://myaccount.google.com/connections and delete your spreadsheet. Your data is immediately removed from RideSheet's access.

docs.title: Google's Privacy Policy
docs.text:
RideSheet operates within Google Workspace. Your use of Google services is also governed by:

  • Google's Privacy Policy: https://policies.google.com/privacy
  • Google's Terms of Service: https://policies.google.com/terms

docs.title: Disclaimer
docs.text:
This privacy policy applies to the official RideSheet project maintained by Full Path LLC. If you modify the code or create a derivative work, you are responsible for your own privacy policy.

Summary: RideSheet keeps your data in your Google account, doesn't share it with third parties (except Google Maps for addresses), and gives you complete control over your information.

docs.title: RideSheet Terms of Service
docs.text:
Service Provider: Full Path LLC

Last Updated: December 31, 2025

docs.title: 1. Acceptance of Terms
docs.text:
By using RideSheet, you agree to these Terms of Service between you and Full Path LLC ("we," "us," "our"). If you do not agree, do not install or use RideSheet.

Who these terms apply to:

  • Individual users who install RideSheet
  • Organizations that deploy RideSheet
  • Anyone who modifies or distributes RideSheet

docs.title: 2. Description of Service
docs.text:
RideSheet is a free, open-source transportation management tool provided by Full Path LLC that operates as a Google Apps Script within Google Sheets. No data is transmitted to or hosted on servers controlled by the RideSheet developers. RideSheet helps you:

  • Manage customer and trip information
  • Create driver manifests
  • Calculate trip distances and times
  • Organize transportation schedules
  • Generate high-quality operations data for reporting and analysis

RideSheet is provided "AS IS" without any warranties or guarantees.

docs.title: 3. Open Source License
docs.text:
RideSheet is open-source software. All code is released under the MIT license.

You are free to:

  • Use RideSheet for any purpose (personal or commercial)
  • Modify the code to suit your needs
  • Distribute original or modified versions
  • Review and audit the source code

Requirements:

  • Include appropriate license notices when distributing
  • Acknowledge that modified versions are not official RideSheet releases. Your modified version must be governed by your own terms of service.
  • Comply with the terms of the open-source license

docs.title: Account Access
docs.text:
• RideSheet requires a Google account to function
  • You must authorize RideSheet to access specific Google services
  • You can revoke access at any time on a per-user basis at https://myaccount.google.com/connections

docs.title: Your Responsibilities
docs.text:
• Maintain the security of your Google account
  • Keep your account credentials confidential
  • Be responsible for all activities under your account
  • Comply with Google's Terms of Service

docs.title: You May Use RideSheet To:
docs.text:
• Manage transportation operations
  • Organize trip and customer data
  • Generate driver manifests
  • Calculate routes and distances
  • Any other lawful transportation management purpose

docs.title: You May NOT Use RideSheet To:
docs.text:
• Violate any applicable laws or regulations
  • Infringe on others' intellectual property rights
  • Harass, abuse, or harm individuals
  • Distribute malware or malicious code
  • Attempt to gain unauthorized access to systems
  • Violate Google's Terms of Service or Acceptable Use Policy

docs.title: Data Ownership
docs.text:
• You own all data you enter into RideSheet
  • RideSheet does not claim any rights to your data
  • Your data stays in your Google account

docs.title: Data Responsibility
docs.text:
You are responsible for:

  • The accuracy of data you enter
  • Compliance with privacy laws (GDPR, CCPA, etc.)
  • Obtaining necessary consents from individuals whose data you store and process
  • Protecting sensitive information appropriately
  • Creating backups of important data

For maximum data control and security, we strongly recommend you only store sensitive data in organizational Google Workspace accounts.

docs.title: Privacy Policy
docs.text: Our Privacy Policy explains how RideSheet accesses and uses your data. By using RideSheet, you also agree to our Privacy Policy.

docs.title: 7. No Warranties
docs.text:
RideSheet is provided "AS IS" and "AS AVAILABLE" without warranties of any kind, either express or implied, including but not limited to:

  • Warranties of merchantability
  • Fitness for a particular purpose
  • Non-infringement
  • Accuracy or reliability
  • Uninterrupted or error-free operation
  • Security or data protection

We do not warrant that:

  • RideSheet will meet your specific requirements
  • RideSheet will be available at all times
  • All errors will be corrected
  • RideSheet is free from viruses or harmful components

docs.title: 8. Limitation of Liability
docs.text: To the maximum extent permitted by law:

docs.title: No Liability for Damages
docs.text:
We are not liable for any indirect, incidental, special, consequential, or punitive damages, including but not limited to:

  • Loss of profits or revenue
  • Loss of data
  • Loss of business opportunities
  • Service interruptions
  • Costs of substitute services

docs.title: Circumstances Include
docs.text:
Even if such damages arise from:

  • Use or inability to use RideSheet
  • Errors or bugs in the software
  • Unauthorized access to your data
  • Third-party actions
  • Any other cause related to RideSheet

docs.title: Maximum Liability
docs.text: If we are found liable despite these limitations, our total liability shall not exceed $100 USD or the amount you paid to use RideSheet (which is $0 for the free version).

docs.title: 9. Indemnification
docs.text:
You agree to indemnify and hold harmless Full Path LLC, its members, employees, contractors, and RideSheet project contributors from any claims, damages, losses, liabilities, and expenses (including legal fees) arising from:

  • Your use or misuse of RideSheet
  • Your violation of these Terms
  • Your violation of any laws or regulations
  • Your violation of third-party rights

docs.title: Google Services
docs.text:
RideSheet integrates with Google services (Sheets, Drive, Docs, Maps). Your use of these services is governed by:

  • Google's Terms of Service
  • Google's Privacy Policy
  • Google Workspace Acceptable Use Policy

We are not responsible for:

  • Changes to Google's services
  • Google service outages
  • Google's terms or policies
  • Google's data practices

docs.title: Google Maps
docs.text:
Address validation and distance calculations use Google Maps API, subject to:

  • Google Maps Platform Terms of Service
  • Google Maps API usage limits
  • Google's privacy policies

docs.title: We May Modify RideSheet
docs.text:
• Add, change, or remove features

  • Change how RideSheet operates

We are not obligated to:

  • Provide updates or support
  • Maintain backward compatibility
  • Notify you of changes
  • Continue developing RideSheet

docs.title: Updates Are Optional
docs.text:
• RideSheet does not auto-update
  • You choose when to update
  • You can continue using older versions
  • You can fork and maintain your own version

docs.title: We May Update These Terms
docs.text:
We reserve the right to modify these Terms at any time.

When we update:

  • The "Last Updated" date will change
  • Material changes will be noted in release notes
  • Continued use after changes constitutes acceptance

Your Options:

  • Review terms periodically
  • Stop using RideSheet if you disagree with changes
  • Fork the project and use your own version

docs.title: No Guaranteed Support
docs.text:
• RideSheet is provided without support obligations
  • We may provide support at our discretion

docs.title: Where to Get Help
docs.text:
• GitHub Issues: https://github.com/full-path/ridesheet/issues
  • Community forums (if available)
